label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The user is asking about the results of the experiment described in the article. The question is about how the students performed. First, I need to recall the details from the text. The study had 143 students split into two groups. One group wrote self-advice in first-person, the other in second-person. They had to do puzzles and then exercise planning. The results said that those using second-person completed more puzzles and had a more positive attitude. Similarly, they also planned to exercise more. Looking at the options: A) First-person had better attitude. But the article says second-person had better attitude and performance. So A is wrong. B) Those who talked to themselves completed most puzzles. Wait, the study had them write, not talk. The question says "talk to themselves" but the article mentions writing. The study didn't have them talking out loud, just writing. So B is incorrect because the instruction was about writing, not talking. C) The ones who used second-person writing were better. That matches the article's findings: they did more puzzles and were more positive.
